title: Data-budget / poll-rate control
description: Owner idea (2026-07-16): a slider or setting letting people probe more or less frequently to tune the data budget vs liveness trade-off. Deferred from v1 because the capped instrument makes the budget self-regulating and the design has no settings surface ('no accounts, no history' — single-purpose instrument). Would need a design-house pass for where it lives.

title: Precision mode: temporarily uncap for a full-speed burst
description: For users who want the real Mbps number: e.g. long-press the dial to run a short full-rate burst (a few seconds, clearly data-labelled) that reports actual uncapped down/up speed, then returns to the capped instrument. Complements the 10/5 Mbps caps recorded in LIMITATIONS. Needs design + consent-copy thought (bursts can move tens of MB).

title: Self-hosted throughput endpoint
description: Replace the Cloudflare __down/__up dependency with a small self-hosted streaming server for tighter control and no third-party dependency (README 'later' item; endpoint already isolated behind HTTPProbeRequest.host). Costs real money/ops — needs-human decision when picked up.

title: Pin SwiftFormat/SwiftLint versions across local + CI
description: CI brew-installs the LATEST swiftformat/swiftlint while local runs whatever's installed — a formatter behavior/CLI change lands as a red CI unrelated to the diff and confuses the loop's escalation (real precedent 2026-07-16: SwiftFormat 0.61 changed CLI arg parsing, breaking the scaffolded `--lint Sources Tests` order). Pin exact versions both sides (mise/Mint, brew pin, or vendored binaries) so the DoD is deterministic.

title: App Store / TestFlight submission prep
description: The backlog builds the app but stops short of shipping it: Apple Developer team + device signing (T025's on-device gate implicitly needs a signed build), App Store Connect record, TestFlight distribution, store metadata + screenshots, age rating, export-compliance answer (ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption already false in project.yml). Mostly needs-human work; convert to gated tasks when the owner wants to ship.

title: Retune caps/bands from real-world device data
description: The capped instrument's constants (down cap 10, up cap 5, Great >=6 / Usable >=2) are pre-real-world guesses; T025 step 7 (walk test on a physical iPhone) collects the data to check them. All constants live in ScoutMeter — one place to retune. Pick this up after T025's worklog records the observations.
